title: rosa glauca var. fugax
intro: Rosa glauca var. fugax, also known as the 'Wild Rose', is a species of shrub in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its fragrant, pale yellow flowers and a blue-gray foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Rosa glauca var. fugax include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
